blocks.body: Reported Commands and Requests shows how to report orders, instructions, advice, and requests with object + infinitive.

{"level":"B1-B2","intro":"Use reported commands and requests when you pass on an order, request, piece of advice, or instruction without a direct quotation.","rules":[{"label":"Object + infinitive","color":"emerald","text":"After many reporting verbs we use the pattern verb + object + to-infinitive.","example":"He told me to sit down."},{"label":"Negative form","color":"blue","text":"For a prohibition or negative instruction, we use not to-infinitive.","example":"She told us not to worry."},{"label":"Choose the right verb","color":"amber","text":"tell, ask, order, and advise are similar, but they do not have the same tone or context.","example":"The doctor advised me to rest."}]}

blocks.heading: 1. Form and structure
blocks.body:
The usual pattern is subject + reporting verb + object + to-infinitive.

  • tell + object + to-infinitive: She told me to call later.
  • ask + object + to-infinitive: He asked us to wait outside.
  • order + object + to-infinitive: The officer ordered them to stop.
  • advise + object + to-infinitive: The teacher advised us to revise.
  • The negative form is object + not to-infinitive.

blocks.heading: 2. Main uses and examples
blocks.body:
Direct sentenceReported versionComment
“Close the door.”He told me to close the door.a normal command
“Please help me with this bag.”She asked him to help her with the bag.a polite request
“Don't touch that wire.”The engineer warned us not to touch that wire.a negative command or warning
“You should take a break.”My friend advised me to take a break.advice

blocks.heading: 3. Common mistakes
blocks.body:
• Mistake: leaving out the object.
    Better: He told me to sit down, not He told to sit down.
  • Mistake: using a that-clause where an infinitive is needed.
    Better: She asked me to wait.
  • Mistake: forgetting not in negative commands.
    Better: They told us not to be late.

blocks.heading: 4. Quick rule
blocks.body:
• For commands and requests, the most common pattern is reporting verb + object + to-infinitive.
  • The negative version is object + not to-infinitive.
  • Choose the reporting verb by meaning: tell for instructions, ask for requests, advise for advice, and order for a strong command.
